project.name: theros-monumental-wsl-toolkit
project.phase: public appliance release / future validation lanes

mission.active_goal: Preserve and validate a reproducible legacy iOS ARMv7 toolchain workflow on WSL/Linux while keeping public/private artifact boundaries clean.
non_goals: Do not claim broad iOS compatibility.
Do not treat host-side stubs as runtime implementations.
Do not pursue behavior-changing tweak logic in this case-study state.
success_criteria: Fresh build/install/verify path remains reproducible for the documented lane.
Allowed claims and not-allowed claims remain explicit.
failure_criteria: Public artifacts leak private SDKs, generated packages, appliance tarballs, credentials, or unsafe local state.
The case study overclaims runtime/device behavior not validated by the LogDoc.

current_state.next_safest_action: Use the sanitized LogDoc and this state file as the v2 case study for Current Breakpoint State handoff behavior.
current_state.do_not_do_next: Do not start device-risky tweak behavior or make broad compatibility claims from this case-study state.

validated: Legacy ARMv7 iOS toolchain build/install/verify workflow is documented for WSL Ubuntu 24.04.
Fresh reproducible toolchain build path is represented in the case study.
Theos wrapper setup and host validation lanes are represented in the case study.
Public sanitized toolchain-only appliance boundary is documented as separate from private SDK-bearing state.
partially_validated: Host-side no-op tweak package mechanics are represented, but this does not prove arbitrary Objective-C/Foundation/Substrate linking correctness or runtime behavior.
not_validated: Broad device/iOS/jailbreak compatibility beyond documented lanes.
Bundled full Theos/iPhoneOS SDK public appliance flow.
Device-side install/uninstall/runtime safety unless explicitly validated in the current LogDoc.
deferred: Further real Objective-C/Foundation/Substrate linking validation unless a safe linker/stub strategy is documented.
Additional device-side validation lanes unless explicitly scoped and risk-bounded.
risk_boundaries: Do not publish private WSL appliance contents.
Do not commit generated packages, logs, SDKs, credentials, or appliance tarballs.
Do not overclaim compatibility outside the documented target matrix.
Do not treat temporary SDK/linker hacks as production validation.
Do not treat host-side package creation as proof of runtime/device behavior.

open_blockers.summary: Old ld64 cannot consume .tbd SDK stubs; meaningful Objective-C/Foundation/Substrate linking requires real Mach-O stubs or an alternate safe linker strategy.

next_session_bootstrap.do_not_start_with: Do not start with device-risky tweak behavior or broad compatibility claims.

allowed_claims: The case study preserves a documented, risk-bounded LogDoc workflow for the described legacy iOS ARMv7 WSL toolchain lane.
The documented host-side build/verification path is represented as the validated project lane.
not_allowed_claims: This supports all legacy iOS versions.
This is proven safe on-device for arbitrary tweaks.
Host-side package creation proves runtime/device behavior.
The public appliance includes Apple SDKs.
